1→"""
     2→Lightweight CLI wrapper for the Agents-eval application.
     3→
     4→This wrapper handles help and basic argument parsing quickly without
     5→loading heavy dependencies. It only imports the main application
     6→when actual processing is needed.
     7→"""
     8→
     9→import argparse
    10→import shutil
    11→from sys import argv, exit
    12→from typing import Any
    13→
    14→from app.config.config_app import OUTPUT_PATH
    15→from app.data_models.app_models import PROVIDER_REGISTRY
    16→
    17→_parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Agents-eval CLI — run MAS evaluation pipeline")
    18→
    19→for _flag, _help in [
    20→    ("--version", "Display version information"),
    21→    ("--include-researcher", "Include the researcher agent"),
    22→    ("--include-analyst", "Include the analyst agent"),
    23→    ("--include-synthesiser", "Include the synthesiser agent"),
    24→    ("--pydantic-ai-stream", "Enable streaming output"),
    25→    ("--download-peerread-full-only", "Download all PeerRead data and exit (setup mode)"),
    26→    ("--download-peerread-samples-only", "Download PeerRead sample and exit (setup mode)"),
    27→    ("--cc-teams", "Use Claude Code Agent Teams mode (requires --engine=cc)"),
    28→    ("--no-llm-suggestions", "Disable LLM-assisted suggestions in generated report"),
    29→]:
    30→    _parser.add_argument(_flag, action="store_true", default=None, help=_help)
    31→
    32→# S8-F6.1: --generate-report and --skip-eval are mutually exclusive
    33→_eval_group = _parser.add_mutually_exclusive_group()
    34→_eval_group.add_argument(
    35→    "--skip-eval",
    36→    action="store_true",
    37→    default=None,
    38→    help="Skip evaluation after run_manager completes",
    39→)
    40→_eval_group.add_argument(
    41→    "--generate-report",
    42→    action="store_true",
    43→    default=None,
    44→    help="Generate a Markdown report after evaluation completes (incompatible with --skip-eval)",
    45→)
    46→
    47→_review_group = _parser.add_mutually_exclusive_group()
    48→_review_group.add_argument(
    49→    "--enable-review-tools",
    50→    action="store_true",
    51→    dest="enable_review_tools",
    52→    help="Enable PeerRead review generation tools (enabled by default)",
    53→)
    54→_review_group.add_argument(
    55→    "--no-review-tools",
    56→    action="store_false",
    57→    dest="enable_review_tools",
    58→    help="Disable PeerRead review generation tools (opt-out)",
    59→)
    60→_parser.set_defaults(enable_review_tools=None)
    61→
    62→_parser.add_argument(
    63→    "--chat-provider",
    64→    choices=sorted(PROVIDER_REGISTRY.keys()),
    65→    help="Specify the chat provider to use",
    66→)
    67→
    68→for _flag, _help in [
    69→    ("--query", "Specify the query to process"),
    70→    ("--chat-config-file", "Path to the chat configuration file"),
    71→    ("--paper-id", "Paper ID for PeerRead review (supports arxiv IDs like '1105.1072')"),
    72→    ("--judge-provider", "Tier 2 LLM judge provider (default: auto, inherits chat provider)"),
    73→    ("--judge-model", "Override Tier 2 judge LLM model (default: inherits chat model when auto)"),
    74→    ("--cc-solo-dir", "Path to CC solo session export directory for baseline comparison"),
    75→    ("--cc-teams-dir", "Path to CC Agent Teams artifacts directory for baseline comparison"),
    76→    ("--cc-teams-tasks-dir", "Path to CC Agent Teams tasks directory (auto-discovered if omitted)"),
    77→]:
    78→    _parser.add_argument(_flag, help=_help)
    79→
    80→_parser.add_argument("--token-limit", type=int, help="Override agent token limit (1000-1000000)")
    81→_parser.add_argument(
    82→    "--peerread-max-papers-per-sample-download",
    83→    type=int,
    84→    help="Max papers to download per split, overrides sample default",
    85→)
    86→_parser.add_argument(
    87→    "--engine",
    88→    default="mas",
    89→    choices=["mas", "cc"],
    90→    help="Execution engine: 'mas' (default) or 'cc' for Claude Code headless",
    91→)
    92→
    93→
    94→def parse_args(argv: list[str]) -> dict[str, Any]:
    95→    """Parse command line arguments into a dictionary.
    96→
    97→    Args:
    98→        argv: List of CLI argument strings (without the program name).
    99→
   100→    Returns:
   101→        Dictionary of explicitly-provided arguments (plus engine default).
   102→
   103→    Example:
   104→        >>> parse_args(["--chat-provider", "ollama", "--include-researcher"])
   105→        {'chat_provider': 'ollama', 'include_researcher': True, 'engine': 'mas'}
   106→    """
   107→    return {k: v for k, v in vars(_parser.parse_args(argv)).items() if v is not None}
   108→
   109→
   110→def _run_cc_engine(args: dict[str, Any], cc_teams: bool) -> Any:
   111→    """Run the Claude Code engine and return the result object.
   112→
   113→    Args:
   114→        args: Parsed CLI arguments dict (mutated: cc_solo_dir may be set).
   115→        cc_teams: Whether to use Agent Teams mode.
   116→
   117→    Returns:
   118→        CCResult object from the engine run.
   119→    """
   120→    from app.engines.cc_engine import build_cc_query, run_cc_solo, run_cc_teams
   121→
   122→    query = build_cc_query(args.get("query", ""), args.get("paper_id"), cc_teams=cc_teams)
   123→    cc_result_obj = (
   124→        run_cc_teams(query, timeout=600) if cc_teams else run_cc_solo(query, timeout=600)
   125→    )
   126→
   127→    if cc_result_obj.session_dir:
   128→        args["cc_solo_dir"] = cc_result_obj.session_dir
   129→
   130→    return cc_result_obj
   131→
   132→
   133→def _maybe_generate_report(result_dict: dict[str, Any], no_llm_suggestions: bool) -> None:
   134→    """Generate and save a Markdown report if composite result is available.
   135→
   136→    Args:
   137→        result_dict: Pipeline result containing composite_result and run_context.
   138→        no_llm_suggestions: Whether to disable LLM-assisted suggestions.
   139→    """
   140→    from datetime import datetime
   141→    from pathlib import Path
   142→
   143→    from app.reports.report_generator import generate_report, save_report
   144→    from app.reports.suggestion_engine import SuggestionEngine
   145→    from app.utils.log import logger
   146→
   147→    composite_result = result_dict.get("composite_result")
   148→    if composite_result is None:
   149→        logger.warning("--generate-report requested but no evaluation result available")
   150→        return
   151→
   152→    engine_obj = SuggestionEngine(no_llm_suggestions=no_llm_suggestions)
   153→    suggestions = engine_obj.generate(composite_result)
   154→    md = generate_report(composite_result, suggestions=suggestions)
   155→
   156→    # Reason: use run_context report_path when available; fall back to output/reports
   157→    run_context = result_dict.get("run_context")
   158→    if run_context is not None:
   159→        output_path = run_context.report_path
   160→    else:
   161→        timestamp = datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%dT%H%M%S")
   162→        output_path = Path(OUTPUT_PATH) / "reports" / f"{timestamp}.md"
   163→
   164→    save_report(md, output_path)
   165→    logger.info(f"Report written to {output_path}")
   166→    print(f"Report saved: {output_path}")
   167→
   168→
   169→def cli_main() -> None:
   170→    """Run the CLI application entry point.
   171→
   172→    Parses arguments, selects the execution engine, runs the pipeline,
   173→    and logs the artifact summary.
   174→    """
   175→    import sys
   176→
   177→    args = parse_args(argv[1:])
   178→    engine = args.pop("engine")
   179→    cc_teams = args.pop("cc_teams", False) or False
   180→    generate_report_flag = args.pop("generate_report", False) or False
   181→    no_llm_suggestions = args.pop("no_llm_suggestions", False) or False
   182→
   183→    # Reason: main() expects a JudgeSettings object, not raw provider/model strings.
   184→    # Mirrors SweepRunner._build_judge_settings() logic.
   185→    judge_provider = args.pop("judge_provider", None)
   186→    judge_model = args.pop("judge_model", None)
   187→    judge_settings = None
   188→    if judge_provider or judge_model:
   189→        from app.judge.evaluation_pipeline import JudgeSettings
   190→
   191→        kwargs: dict[str, Any] = {}
   192→        if judge_provider:
   193→            kwargs["tier2_provider"] = judge_provider
   194→        if judge_model:
   195→            kwargs["tier2_model"] = judge_model
   196→        judge_settings = JudgeSettings(**kwargs)
   197→    args["judge_settings"] = judge_settings
   198→
   199→    if engine == "cc" and not shutil.which("claude"):
   200→        print(
   201→            "error: --engine=cc requires the 'claude' CLI to be installed and on PATH",
   202→            file=sys.stderr,
   203→        )
   204→        exit(1)
   205→
   206→    from asyncio import run
   207→
   208→    from app.app import main
   209→    from app.utils.artifact_registry import get_artifact_registry
   210→    from app.utils.log import logger
   211→
   212→    logger.info(f"Used arguments: {args}")
   213→
   214→    cc_result_obj = _run_cc_engine(args, cc_teams) if engine == "cc" else None
   215→
   216→    try:
   217→        result_dict = run(main(**args, engine=engine, cc_result=cc_result_obj, cc_teams=cc_teams))
   218→        if generate_report_flag and result_dict:
   219→            _maybe_generate_report(result_dict, no_llm_suggestions)
   220→    finally:
   221→        logger.info(get_artifact_registry().format_summary_block())
   222→
   223→
   224→if __name__ == "__main__":
   225→    cli_main()
   226→

1→"""
     2→Main entry point for the Agents-eval application.
     3→
     4→This module initializes the agentic system, loads configuration files,
     5→handles user input, and orchestrates the multi-agent workflow using
     6→asynchronous execution. It integrates logging, tracing, and authentication,
     7→and supports both CLI and programmatic execution.
     8→
     9→Evaluation orchestration is delegated to app.judge.evaluation_runner.
    10→"""
    11→
    12→from __future__ import annotations
    13→
    14→import uuid as _uuid
    15→from collections.abc import Callable
    16→from pathlib import Path
    17→from typing import Any, TypeVar, cast
    18→
    19→from logfire import span
    20→
    21→# Reason: weave is optional - only import if available (requires WANDB_API_KEY)
    22→try:
    23→    from weave import op  # type: ignore[reportMissingImports]
    24→except ImportError:
    25→    # Fallback: no-op decorator when weave not installed
    26→    from typing import Any
    27→
    28→    _T = TypeVar("_T", bound=Callable[..., Any])
    29→
    30→    def op() -> Callable[[_T], _T]:  # type: ignore[reportRedeclaration]
    31→        """No-op decorator fallback when weave is unavailable."""
    32→
    33→        def decorator(func: _T) -> _T:
    34→            return func
    35→
    36→        return decorator
    37→
    38→
    39→from app.__init__ import __version__
    40→from app.agents.agent_system import (
    41→    get_manager,
    42→    initialize_logfire_instrumentation_from_settings,
    43→    run_manager,
    44→    setup_agent_env,
    45→)
    46→from app.config.app_env import AppEnv
    47→from app.config.config_app import (
    48→    CHAT_CONFIG_FILE,
    49→    CHAT_DEFAULT_PROVIDER,
    50→    DEFAULT_REVIEW_PROMPT_TEMPLATE,
    51→    PROJECT_NAME,
    52→)
    53→from app.config.judge_settings import JudgeSettings
    54→from app.data_models.app_models import ChatConfig
    55→from app.data_utils.datasets_peerread import (
    56→    download_peerread_dataset,
    57→)
    58→from app.judge.evaluation_runner import (
    59→    build_graph_from_trace as _build_graph_from_trace,
    60→)
    61→from app.judge.evaluation_runner import (
    62→    run_evaluation_if_enabled as _run_evaluation_if_enabled,
    63→)
    64→from app.judge.graph_export import persist_graph
    65→from app.utils.error_messages import generic_exception
    66→from app.utils.load_configs import load_config
    67→from app.utils.log import logger
    68→from app.utils.login import login
    69→from app.utils.paths import resolve_config_path
    70→from app.utils.run_context import RunContext, get_active_run_context, set_active_run_context
    71→
    72→CONFIG_FOLDER = "config"
    73→
    74→
    75→def _resolve_engine_type(engine: str, cc_teams: bool) -> str:
    76→    """Map engine name and cc_teams flag to engine_type for RunContext.
    77→
    78→    Args:
    79→        engine: Engine identifier ('mas' or 'cc').
    80→        cc_teams: Whether CC teams mode is active.
    81→
    82→    Returns:
    83→        Engine type string: 'mas', 'cc_solo', or 'cc_teams'.
    84→    """
    85→    if engine == "cc":
    86→        return "cc_teams" if cc_teams else "cc_solo"
    87→    return "mas"
    88→
    89→
    90→async def _run_agent_execution(
    91→    chat_config_file: str | Path,
    92→    chat_provider: str,
    93→    query: str,
    94→    paper_id: str | None,
    95→    enable_review_tools: bool,
    96→    include_researcher: bool,
    97→    include_analyst: bool,
    98→    include_synthesiser: bool,
    99→    token_limit: int | None,
   100→    execution_id: str | None = None,
   101→) -> tuple[str, dict[str, str], Any, str]:
   102→    """Execute agent system and return execution ID, prompts, manager output, and chat model.
   103→
   104→    Args:
   105→        chat_config_file: Path to chat configuration file.
   106→        chat_provider: LLM provider name.
   107→        query: User query string.
   108→        paper_id: Optional PeerRead paper ID.
   109→        enable_review_tools: Whether to enable review tools.
   110→        include_researcher: Whether to include researcher agent.
   111→        include_analyst: Whether to include analyst agent.
   112→        include_synthesiser: Whether to include synthesiser agent.
   113→        token_limit: Optional token limit override.
   114→        execution_id: Optional pre-generated execution ID forwarded to run_manager.
   115→
   116→    Returns:
   117→        Tuple of (execution_id, prompts dict, manager_output, chat_model).
   118→    """
   119→    chat_config = load_config(chat_config_file, ChatConfig)
   120→    prompts: dict[str, str] = cast(dict[str, str], chat_config.prompts)  # type: ignore[reportUnknownMemberType]
   121→
   122→    query, review_tools_enabled = _prepare_query(paper_id, query, prompts)
   123→    enable_review_tools = enable_review_tools or review_tools_enabled
   124→
   125→    chat_env_config = AppEnv()
   126→    agent_env = setup_agent_env(chat_provider, query, chat_config, chat_env_config, token_limit)
   127→
   128→    login(PROJECT_NAME, chat_env_config)
   129→    _initialize_instrumentation()
   130→
   131→    manager = get_manager(
   132→        agent_env.provider,
   133→        agent_env.provider_config,
   134→        agent_env.api_key,
   135→        agent_env.prompts,
   136→        include_researcher,
   137→        include_analyst,
   138→        include_synthesiser,
   139→        enable_review_tools=enable_review_tools,
   140→    )
   141→    execution_id, manager_output = await run_manager(
   142→        manager,
   143→        agent_env.query,
   144→        agent_env.provider,
   145→        agent_env.usage_limits,
   146→        execution_id=execution_id,
   147→    )
   148→
   149→    return execution_id, prompts, manager_output, agent_env.provider_config.model_name
   150→
   151→
   152→def _handle_download_mode(
   153→    download_full: bool, download_samples: bool, max_samples: int | None
   154→) -> bool:
   155→    """Handle dataset download modes. Returns True if download was performed."""
   156→    if download_full:
   157→        logger.info("Full download-only mode activated")
   158→        try:
   159→            download_peerread_dataset(peerread_max_papers_per_sample_download=None)
   160→            logger.info("Setup completed successfully. Exiting.")
   161→            return True
   162→        except Exception as e:
   163→            logger.error(f"Setup failed: {e}")
   164→            raise
   165→
   166→    if download_samples:
   167→        logger.info(f"Downloading only {max_samples} samples")
   168→        try:
   169→            download_peerread_dataset(max_samples)
   170→            logger.info("Setup completed successfully. Exiting.")
   171→            return True
   172→        except Exception as e:
   173→            logger.error(f"Setup failed: {e}")
   174→            raise
   175→
   176→    return False
   177→
   178→
   179→def _initialize_instrumentation() -> None:
   180→    """Initialize Logfire instrumentation if enabled in settings."""
   181→    judge_settings = JudgeSettings()
   182→    if judge_settings.logfire_enabled:
   183→        initialize_logfire_instrumentation_from_settings(judge_settings)
   184→
   185→
   186→def _prepare_query(paper_id: str | None, query: str, prompts: dict[str, str]) -> tuple[str, bool]:
   187→    """Prepare query and determine if review tools should be enabled."""
   188→    if paper_id:
   189→        if not query:
   190→            paper_review_template = prompts.get(
   191→                "paper_review_query", DEFAULT_REVIEW_PROMPT_TEMPLATE
   192→            )
   193→            query = paper_review_template.format(paper_id=paper_id)
   194→        logger.info(f"Paper review mode enabled for paper {paper_id}")
   195→        return query, True
   196→
   197→    if not query:
   198→        default_prompt = prompts.get("default_query", "What would you like to research? ")
   199→        query = input(f"{default_prompt} ")
   200→
   201→    return query, False
   202→
   203→
   204→def _prepare_result_dict(
   205→    composite_result: Any | None,
   206→    graph: Any | None,
   207→    execution_id: str | None = None,
   208→    run_context: RunContext | None = None,
   209→) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
   210→    """Prepare result dictionary for GUI usage.
   211→
   212→    Args:
   213→        composite_result: Evaluation result
   214→        graph: Interaction graph
   215→        execution_id: Execution trace ID for display on Evaluation page
   216→        run_context: Optional per-run context for artifact paths
   217→
   218→    Returns:
   219→        Dict with result, graph, execution_id, and run_context if available, None otherwise
   220→    """
   221→    # Return dict if we have either result or graph
   222→    if composite_result is not None or graph is not None:
   223→        return {
   224→            "composite_result": composite_result,
   225→            "graph": graph,
   226→            # S8-F8.2: include execution_id for Evaluation Results page threading
   227→            "execution_id": execution_id,
   228→            "run_context": run_context,
   229→        }
   230→    return None
   231→
   232→
   233→@op()  # type: ignore[reportUntypedFunctionDecorator]
   234→def _extract_cc_artifacts(cc_result: Any) -> tuple[str, Any, Any]:
   235→    """Extract execution ID, graph, and trace data from a CC engine result.
   236→
   237→    Args:
   238→        cc_result: CCResult from solo or teams execution.
   239→
   240→    Returns:
   241→        Tuple of (execution_id, interaction_graph, graph_trace).
   242→    """
   243→    from app.engines.cc_engine import cc_result_to_graph_trace
   244→    from app.judge.graph_builder import build_interaction_graph
   245→
   246→    graph_trace = cc_result_to_graph_trace(cc_result)
   247→    return cc_result.execution_id, build_interaction_graph(graph_trace), graph_trace
   248→
   249→
   250→async def _run_cc_engine_path(
   251→    cc_result: Any,
   252→    skip_eval: bool,
   253→    paper_id: str | None,
   254→    cc_solo_dir: str | None,
   255→    cc_teams_dir: str | None,
   256→    cc_teams_tasks_dir: str | None,
   257→    chat_provider: str,
   258→    judge_settings: JudgeSettings | None,
   259→    cc_teams: bool = False,
   260→    run_dir: Path | None = None,
   261→    cc_model: str | None = None,
   262→) -> tuple[Any, Any, str | None]:
   263→    """Execute CC engine path: extract artifacts, evaluate, set engine_type.
   264→
   265→    Args:
   266→        cc_result: CCResult from solo or teams execution.
   267→        skip_eval: Whether to skip evaluation.
   268→        paper_id: Optional PeerRead paper ID.
   269→        cc_solo_dir: CC solo trace directory.
   270→        cc_teams_dir: CC teams trace directory.
   271→        cc_teams_tasks_dir: CC teams tasks directory.
   272→        chat_provider: LLM provider name.
   273→        judge_settings: Optional judge settings.
   274→        cc_teams: Whether CC was run in teams mode (source of truth for engine_type).
   275→        run_dir: Per-run output directory from up-front RunContext.
   276→        cc_model: CC model name, forwarded as chat_model to evaluation pipeline.

    50→def _perform_downloads(
    51→    downloader: "PeerReadDownloader",
    52→    config: PeerReadConfig,
    53→    max_papers: int,
    54→) -> tuple[int, list[str]]:
    55→    """Perform downloads for all venue/split combinations.
    56→
    57→    Args:
    58→        downloader: PeerReadDownloader instance.
    59→        config: PeerRead dataset configuration.
    60→        max_papers: Maximum number of papers to download per venue/split.
    61→
    62→    Returns:
    63→        Tuple of (total_downloaded, failed_downloads).
    64→    """
    65→    total_downloaded = 0
    66→    failed_downloads: list[str] = []
    67→
    68→    for venue in config.venues:
    69→        for split in config.splits:
    70→            logger.info(f"Downloading {venue}/{split}...")
    71→            result = downloader.download_venue_split(venue, split, max_papers=max_papers)
    72→
    73→            if result.success:
    74→                logger.info(f"✓ {venue}/{split}: {result.papers_downloaded} downloaded")
    75→                total_downloaded += result.papers_downloaded
    76→            else:
    77→                error_msg = f"✗ {venue}/{split}: {result.error_message}"
    78→                logger.error(error_msg)
    79→                failed_downloads.append(f"{venue}/{split}")
    80→
    81→    return total_downloaded, failed_downloads
    82→
    83→
    84→def _verify_downloads(
    85→    loader: "PeerReadLoader",
    86→    config: PeerReadConfig,
    87→    failed_downloads: list[str],
    88→) -> int:
    89→    """Verify downloads by attempting to load papers.
    90→
    91→    Args:
    92→        loader: PeerReadLoader instance.
    93→        config: PeerRead dataset configuration.
    94→        failed_downloads: List to append verification failures to.
    95→
    96→    Returns:
    97→        Number of papers verified.
    98→    """
    99→    logger.info("Verifying download integrity...")
   100→    verification_count = 0
   101→
   102→    for venue in config.venues:
   103→        for split in config.splits:
   104→            try:
   105→                papers = loader.load_papers(venue, split)
   106→                verification_count += len(papers)
   107→                logger.info(f"✓ Verified {venue}/{split}: {len(papers)} papers loaded")
   108→            except Exception as e:
   109→                logger.error(f"✗ Verification failed for {venue}/{split}: {e}")
   110→                failed_downloads.append(f"{venue}/{split} (verification)")
   111→
   112→    return verification_count
   113→
   114→
   115→def _validate_download_results(
   116→    total_downloaded: int,
   117→    verification_count: int,
   118→    failed_downloads: list[str],
   119→) -> None:
   120→    """Validate download results and raise if failures occurred.
   121→
   122→    Args:
   123→        total_downloaded: Number of papers downloaded.
   124→        verification_count: Number of papers verified.
   125→        failed_downloads: List of failed download/verification items.
   126→
   127→    Raises:
   128→        Exception: If download or verification failed.
   129→    """
   130→    if failed_downloads:
   131→        logger.warning(f"Failed downloads/verifications: {failed_downloads}")
   132→        logger.warning("Some downloads failed, but continuing (this may be expected)")
   133→        raise Exception(f"Failed to download from {len(failed_downloads)} sources.")
   134→
   135→    if total_downloaded == 0 and verification_count == 0:
   136→        raise Exception("No papers were downloaded or verified successfully")

   139→def download_peerread_dataset(
   140→    peerread_max_papers_per_sample_download: int | None = None,
   141→) -> None:
   142→    """
   143→    Download PeerRead dataset and verify the download.
   144→
   145→    This function handles the setup phase separately from MAS execution,
   146→    following Separation of Concerns principle. It downloads the dataset
   147→    to the configured path and verifies the download was successful.
   148→
   149→    Args:
   150→        peerread_max_papers_per_sample_download: The maximum number of papers to
   151→            download. If None, downloads all papers it can find.
   152→
   153→    Raises:
   154→        Exception: If download or verification fails.
   155→    """
   156→    logger.info("Starting PeerRead dataset download (setup mode)")
   157→
   158→    try:
   159→        config = load_peerread_config()
   160→        logger.info(
   161→            f"Loaded PeerRead config: {len(config.venues)} venues, {len(config.splits)} splits"
   162→        )
   163→
   164→        downloader = PeerReadDownloader(config)
   165→        logger.info(f"Download target directory: {downloader.cache_dir}")
   166→
   167→        max_papers = (
   168→            peerread_max_papers_per_sample_download
   169→            if peerread_max_papers_per_sample_download is not None
   170→            else config.max_papers_per_query
   171→        )
   172→
   173→        total_downloaded, failed_downloads = _perform_downloads(downloader, config, max_papers)
   174→
   175→        loader = PeerReadLoader(config)
   176→        verification_count = _verify_downloads(loader, config, failed_downloads)
   177→
   178→        logger.info("=== Download Summary ===")
   179→        logger.info(f"Total papers downloaded: {total_downloaded}")
   180→        logger.info(f"Total papers verified: {verification_count}")
   181→        logger.info(f"Download directory: {downloader.cache_dir}")
   182→
   183→        _validate_download_results(total_downloaded, verification_count, failed_downloads)
   184→
   185→        logger.info("✓ PeerRead dataset download and verification completed successfully")
   186→
   187→    except Exception as e:
   188→        error_msg = f"PeerRead dataset download failed: {e}"
   189→        logger.error(error_msg)
   190→        raise Exception(error_msg) from e

   215→class PeerReadDownloader:
   216→    """Downloads PeerRead dataset files with caching and validation.
   217→
   218→    Handles direct download from GitHub repository with progress tracking,
   219→    error recovery, and integrity verification.
   220→    """
   221→
   222→    def __init__(self, config: PeerReadConfig):
   223→        """Initialize downloader with configuration.
   224→
   225→        Args:
   226→            config: PeerRead dataset configuration.
   227→        """
   228→        self.config = config
   229→        # Resolve cache directory relative to project root
   230→        self.cache_dir = resolve_project_path(config.cache_directory)
   231→        headers: dict[str, str] = {}
   232→        app_env = AppEnv()
   233→        if app_env.GITHUB_API_KEY:
   234→            logger.info("Using GitHub API key for authenticated requests")
   235→            headers["Authorization"] = f"token {app_env.GITHUB_API_KEY}"
   236→        self.client = Client(headers=headers)
   237→
   238→    def _construct_url(
   239→        self,
   240→        venue: str,
   241→        split: str,
   242→        data_type: str,
   243→        paper_id: str,
   244→    ) -> str:
   245→        """Construct download URL for specific file.
   246→
   247→        Args:
   248→            venue: Conference venue (e.g., 'acl_2017').
   249→            split: Data split ('train', 'test', 'dev').
   250→            data_type: Type of data ('reviews', 'parsed_pdfs', 'pdfs').
   251→            paper_id: Unique paper identifier.
   252→
   253→        Returns:
   254→            Complete download URL.
   255→
   256→        Raises:
   257→            ValueError: If venue or split is invalid.
   258→        """
   259→        if venue not in self.config.venues:
   260→            raise ValueError(f"Invalid venue: {venue}. Valid venues: {self.config.venues}")
   261→
   262→        if split not in self.config.splits:
   263→            raise ValueError(f"Invalid split: {split}. Valid splits: {self.config.splits}")
   264→
   265→        if data_type not in DATA_TYPE_SPECS:
   266→            raise ValueError(
   267→                f"Invalid data_type: {data_type}. Valid types: {sorted(DATA_TYPE_SPECS)}"
   268→            )
   269→
   270→        spec = DATA_TYPE_SPECS[data_type]
   271→        filename = f"{paper_id}{spec.extension}"
   272→        return f"{self.config.raw_github_base_url}/{venue}/{split}/{data_type}/{filename}"
   273→
   274→    def _extract_paper_id_from_filename(
   275→        self,
   276→        filename: str,
   277→        data_type: str,
   278→    ) -> str | None:
   279→        """Extract paper ID from filename based on data type.
   280→
   281→        Args:
   282→            filename: Name of the file.
   283→            data_type: Type of data ('reviews', 'parsed_pdfs', 'pdfs').
   284→
   285→        Returns:
   286→            Paper ID without extension, or None if filename doesn't match.
   287→        """
   288→        spec = DATA_TYPE_SPECS.get(data_type)
   289→        if spec is None or not filename.endswith(spec.extension):
   290→            return None
   291→        return filename[: -len(spec.extension)]
   292→
   293→    def _discover_available_files(
   294→        self,
   295→        venue: str,
   296→        split: str,
   297→        data_type: str,
   298→    ) -> list[str]:
   299→        """Discover available files in a GitHub repository directory.
   300→
   301→        Args:
   302→            venue: Conference venue (e.g., 'acl_2017').
   303→            split: Data split ('train', 'test', 'dev').
   304→            data_type: Type of data ('reviews', 'parsed_pdfs', 'pdfs').
   305→
   306→        Returns:
   307→            List of paper IDs (without extensions) available in the directory.
   308→        """
   309→        api_url = f"{self.config.github_api_base_url}/{venue}/{split}/{data_type}"
   310→
   311→        try:
   312→            # Validate URL for SSRF protection (CVE-2026-25580 mitigation)
   313→            validated_url = validate_url(api_url)
   314→            logger.info(f"Discovering {data_type} files in {venue}/{split} via GitHub API")
   315→            response = self.client.get(validated_url, timeout=self.config.download_timeout)
   316→            response.raise_for_status()
   317→
   318→            files_data = response.json()
   319→
   320→            paper_ids: list[str] = []
   321→            for file_info in files_data:
   322→                if file_info.get("type") != "file":
   323→                    continue
   324→
   325→                filename = file_info.get("name", "")
   326→                paper_id = self._extract_paper_id_from_filename(filename, data_type)
   327→                if paper_id:
   328→                    paper_ids.append(paper_id)
   329→
   330→            logger.info(f"Found {len(paper_ids)} {data_type} files in {venue}/{split}")
   331→            return sorted(paper_ids)
   332→
   333→        except (RequestError, HTTPStatusError) as e:
   334→            logger.error(f"Failed to discover {data_type} files for {venue}/{split}: {e}")
   335→            return []
   336→        except (KeyError, ValueError) as e:
   337→            logger.error(
   338→                f"Failed to parse GitHub API response for {venue}/{split}/{data_type}: {e}"
   339→            )
   340→            return []
   341→
   342→    def _handle_download_error(
   343→        self,
   344→        error: Exception,
   345→        data_type: str,
   346→        paper_id: str,
   347→    ) -> bool:
   348→        """Handle download errors and determine if retry should continue.
   349→
   350→        Args:
   351→            error: The exception that occurred.
   352→            data_type: Type of data being downloaded.
   353→            paper_id: Paper identifier.
   354→
   355→        Returns:
   356→            True if retry should continue, False otherwise.
   357→        """
   358→        if isinstance(error, HTTPStatusError) and error.response.status_code == 429:
   359→            logger.warning(
   360→                f"Rate limit hit for {data_type}/{paper_id}. "
   361→                f"Retrying in {self.config.retry_delay_seconds} seconds..."
   362→            )
   363→            sleep(self.config.retry_delay_seconds)
   364→            return True
   365→
   366→        logger.error(f"Failed to download {data_type}/{paper_id}: {error}")
   367→        return False
   368→
   369→    def download_file(
   370→        self,
   371→        venue: str,
   372→        split: str,
   373→        data_type: str,
   374→        paper_id: str,
   375→    ) -> bytes | dict[str, Any] | None:
   376→        """Download a single file.
   377→
   378→        Args:
   379→            venue: Conference venue.
   380→            split: Data split.
   381→            data_type: Type of data ('reviews', 'parsed_pdfs', 'pdfs').
   382→            paper_id: Paper identifier.
   383→
   384→        Returns:
   385→            File content (JSON dict for .json files, bytes for PDFs),
   386→            or None if download fails.
   387→
   388→        Raises:
   389→            ValueError: If venue/split is invalid.
   390→        """
   391→        url = self._construct_url(venue, split, data_type, paper_id)
   392→
   393→        for attempt in range(self.config.max_retries):
   394→            try:
   395→                # Validate URL for SSRF protection (CVE-2026-25580 mitigation)
   396→                validated_url = validate_url(url)
   397→                logger.info(
   398→                    f"Downloading {data_type}/{paper_id} from {validated_url} "
   399→                    f"(Attempt {attempt + 1}/{self.config.max_retries})"
   400→                )
   401→
   402→                response = self.client.get(validated_url, timeout=self.config.download_timeout)
   403→                response.raise_for_status()
   404→
   405→                if DATA_TYPE_SPECS[data_type].is_json:
   406→                    return response.json()
   407→                return response.content
   408→
   409→            except (HTTPStatusError, RequestError, JSONDecodeError) as e:
   410→                should_retry = self._handle_download_error(e, data_type, paper_id)
   411→                if not should_retry:
   412→                    return None
   413→
   414→        logger.error(
   415→            f"Failed to download {data_type}/{paper_id} after {self.config.max_retries} attempts."
   416→        )
   417→        return None
   418→
   419→    def _get_cache_filename(self, data_type: str, paper_id: str) -> str:
   420→        """Get cache filename for given data type and paper ID.
   421→
   422→        Args:
   423→            data_type: Type of data ('reviews', 'parsed_pdfs', 'pdfs').
   424→            paper_id: Paper identifier.
   425→
   426→        Returns:
   427→            Cache filename.
   428→        """
   429→        if data_type not in DATA_TYPE_SPECS:
   430→            raise ValueError(
   431→                f"Invalid data_type: {data_type}. Valid types: {sorted(DATA_TYPE_SPECS)}"
   432→            )
   433→        return f"{paper_id}{DATA_TYPE_SPECS[data_type].extension}"
   434→
   435→    def _save_file_data(
   436→        self,
   437→        file_data: bytes | dict[str, Any],
   438→        cache_file: Path,
   439→        data_type: str,
   440→    ) -> None:
   441→        """Save downloaded file data to cache.
   442→
   443→        Args:
   444→            file_data: Downloaded file content.
   445→            cache_file: Path to cache file.
   446→            data_type: Type of data being saved.
   447→        """
   448→        spec = DATA_TYPE_SPECS.get(data_type)
   449→        if spec is not None and spec.is_json:
   450→            with open(cache_file, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   451→                dump(file_data, f, indent=2)
   452→        elif isinstance(file_data, bytes):
   453→            with open(cache_file, "wb") as f:
   454→                f.write(file_data)
   455→
   456→    def _download_single_data_type(
   457→        self,
   458→        venue: str,
   459→        split: str,
   460→        data_type: str,
   461→        paper_id: str,
   462→        base_cache_path: Path,
   463→        errors: list[str],
   464→    ) -> bool:
   465→        """Download a single data type for a paper.
   466→
   467→        Args:
   468→            venue: Conference venue.
   469→            split: Data split.
   470→            data_type: Type of data to download.
   471→            paper_id: Paper identifier.
   472→            base_cache_path: Base cache directory path.
   473→            errors: List to append errors to.
   474→
   475→        Returns:
   476→            True if file was downloaded or already cached, False otherwise.
   477→        """
   478→        data_type_path = base_cache_path / data_type
   479→        data_type_path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   480→
   481→        cache_filename = self._get_cache_filename(data_type, paper_id)
   482→        if not cache_filename:
   483→            return False
   484→
   485→        cache_file = data_type_path / cache_filename
   486→
   487→        if cache_file.exists():
   488→            logger.debug(f"{data_type}/{paper_id} already cached")
   489→            return True
   490→
   491→        file_data = self.download_file(venue, split, data_type, paper_id)
   492→        if file_data is None:
   493→            errors.append(f"Failed to download {data_type}/{paper_id}")
   494→            return False
   495→
   496→        self._save_file_data(file_data, cache_file, data_type)
   497→        logger.info(f"Cached {data_type}/{paper_id}")
   498→        return True
   499→
   500→    def _download_paper_all_types(
   501→        self,
   502→        venue: str,
   503→        split: str,
   504→        paper_id: str,
   505→        base_cache_path: Path,
   506→        errors: list[str],
   507→    ) -> bool:
   508→        """Download all data types for a single paper.
   509→
   510→        Args:
   511→            venue: Conference venue.
   512→            split: Data split.
   513→            paper_id: Paper identifier.
   514→            base_cache_path: Base cache directory path.
   515→            errors: List to append errors to.
   516→
   517→        Returns:
   518→            True if at least one file was downloaded successfully.
   519→        """
   520→        data_types = ["reviews", "parsed_pdfs", "pdfs"]
   521→        paper_downloaded = False
   522→
   523→        for data_type in data_types:
   524→            success = self._download_single_data_type(
   525→                venue, split, data_type, paper_id, base_cache_path, errors
   526→            )
   527→            if success and not paper_downloaded:
   528→                paper_downloaded = True
   529→
   530→        return paper_downloaded

   532→    def download_venue_split(
   533→        self,
   534→        venue: str,
   535→        split: str,
   536→        max_papers: int | None = None,
   537→    ) -> DownloadResult:
   538→        """Download all files for a venue/split combination across all data types.
   539→
   540→        Args:
   541→            venue: Conference venue.
   542→            split: Data split.
   543→            max_papers: Maximum number of papers to download.
   544→
   545→        Returns:
   546→            DownloadResult with download statistics.
   547→        """
   548→        base_cache_path = self.cache_dir / venue / split
   549→        available_paper_ids = self._discover_available_files(venue, split, "reviews")
   550→
   551→        if not available_paper_ids:
   552→            error_msg = f"No review files discovered for {venue}/{split}"
   553→            logger.error(error_msg)
   554→            return DownloadResult(
   555→                success=False,
   556→                cache_path=str(base_cache_path),
   557→                papers_downloaded=0,
   558→                error_message=error_msg,
   559→            )
   560→
   561→        max_papers = max_papers or self.config.max_papers_per_query
   562→        paper_ids_to_download = available_paper_ids[:max_papers]
   563→        logger.info(
   564→            f"Will download {len(paper_ids_to_download)} of "
   565→            f"{len(available_paper_ids)} available papers across all data types"
   566→        )
   567→
   568→        downloaded = 0
   569→        errors: list[str] = []
   570→
   571→        for paper_id in paper_ids_to_download:
   572→            if self._download_paper_all_types(venue, split, paper_id, base_cache_path, errors):
   573→                downloaded += 1
   574→
   575→        success = downloaded > 0
   576→        error_message = None if success else "; ".join(errors[:5])
   577→
   578→        return DownloadResult(
   579→            success=success,
   580→            cache_path=str(base_cache_path),
   581→            papers_downloaded=downloaded,
   582→            error_message=error_message,
   583→        )

   589→    def __init__(self, config: PeerReadConfig | None = None):
   590→        """Initialize loader with configuration.

   599→    def _extract_text_from_parsed_data(self, parsed_data: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
   600→        """Extract text content from parsed PDF data.
   601→
   602→        Args:
   603→            parsed_data: Parsed PDF JSON data.
   604→
   605→        Returns:
   606→            Concatenated text from all sections.
   607→        """
   608→        full_text: list[str] = []
   609→        sections = parsed_data.get("metadata", {}).get("sections", [])
   610→        for section in sections:
   611→            if "text" in section:
   612→                full_text.append(section["text"])
   613→        return "\n".join(full_text).strip()

   615→    def _load_parsed_file(self, parsed_file: Path) -> str | None:
   616→        """Load and parse a single parsed PDF file.
   617→
   618→        Args:
   619→            parsed_file: Path to parsed PDF file.
   620→
   621→        Returns:
   622→            Extracted text content, or None if loading fails.
   623→        """
   624→        try:
   625→            with open(parsed_file, encoding="utf-8") as f:
   626→                parsed_data = load(f)
   627→            return self._extract_text_from_parsed_data(parsed_data)
   628→        except Exception as e:

   632→    def _find_parsed_pdf_in_split(
   633→        self,
   634→        venue: str,
   635→        split: str,
   636→        paper_id: str,
   637→    ) -> str | None:
   638→        """Find and load parsed PDF content in a specific venue/split.

   693→    def _create_review_from_dict(self, review_data: dict[str, Any]) -> PeerReadReview:
   694→        """Create PeerReadReview from dictionary with optional field handling.

   696→        Args:

   704→    def _validate_papers(
   705→        self,
   706→        papers_data: list[dict[str, Any]],
   707→    ) -> list[PeerReadPaper]:
   708→        """Validate and convert paper data to Pydantic models.
   709→
   710→        Args:
   711→            papers_data: List of paper dictionaries.
   712→
   713→        Returns:
   714→            List of validated PeerReadPaper models.
   715→        """
   716→        validated_papers: list[PeerReadPaper] = []
   717→        skipped_ids: list[str] = []
   718→
   719→        for paper_data in papers_data:
   720→            try:
   721→                # Convert from PeerRead format to our model format
   722→                paper_id = str(paper_data.get("id", "unknown"))
   723→                reviews: list[PeerReadReview] = [
   724→                    self._create_review_from_dict(r) for r in paper_data.get("reviews", [])
   725→                ]
   726→
   727→                paper = PeerReadPaper(
   728→                    paper_id=paper_id,
   729→                    title=paper_data["title"],
   730→                    abstract=paper_data["abstract"],
   731→                    reviews=reviews,
   732→                    review_histories=[
   733→                        " ".join(map(str, h)) for h in paper_data.get("histories", [])
   734→                    ],
   735→                )
   736→
   737→                # Exclude papers where any review is missing required score fields
   738→                if reviews and not all(r.is_compliant() for r in reviews):
   739→                    skipped_ids.append(paper_id)
   740→                    continue
   741→
   742→                validated_papers.append(paper)
   743→
   744→            except Exception as e:
   745→                logger.warning(f"Failed to validate paper {paper_data.get('id', 'unknown')}: {e}")
   746→                continue
   747→
   748→        if skipped_ids:
   749→            logger.info(
   750→                f"Skipping {len(skipped_ids)} non-compliant papers "
   751→                f"(missing required score fields): {', '.join(skipped_ids)}"
   752→            )
   753→
   754→        return validated_papers

   756→    def load_papers(
   757→        self,
   758→        venue: str = "acl_2017",
   759→        split: str = "train",
   760→    ) -> list[PeerReadPaper]:
   761→        """Load papers from cached data or download if needed.
   762→
   763→        Args:
   764→            venue: Conference venue.
   765→            split: Data split.
   766→
   767→        Returns:
   768→            List of validated PeerReadPaper models.
   769→
   770→        Raises:
   771→            FileNotFoundError: If cache directory doesn't exist and download fails.
   772→        """
   773→        cache_path = self.cache_dir / venue / split
   774→
   775→        if not cache_path.exists():
   776→            error_msg = (
   777→                f"PeerRead dataset not found for {venue}/{split}. "
   778→                f"Please download the dataset first using: "
   779→                f"'python src/app/main.py --download-peerread-only' or "
   780→                f"'make app_cli ARGS=\"--download-peerread-only\"'"
   781→            )
   782→            logger.error(error_msg)
   783→            raise FileNotFoundError(error_msg)
   784→
   785→        # Load all cached papers from reviews directory
   786→        reviews_path = cache_path / "reviews"
   787→
   788→        if not reviews_path.exists():
   789→            error_msg = (
   790→                f"PeerRead reviews not found for {venue}/{split}. "
   791→                f"Please download the dataset first using: "
   792→                f"'python src/app/main.py --download-peerread-only' or "
   793→                f"'make app_cli ARGS=\"--download-peerread-only\"'"
   794→            )
   795→            logger.error(error_msg)
   796→            raise FileNotFoundError(error_msg)
   797→
   798→        papers_data: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
   799→        for json_file in reviews_path.glob("*.json"):
   800→            try:
   801→                with open(json_file, encoding="utf-8") as f:
   802→                    papers_data.append(load(f))
   803→            except Exception as e:

   807→        return self._validate_papers(papers_data)

   809→    def _load_paper_from_path(self, cache_path: Path, paper_id: str) -> PeerReadPaper | None:

   812→        Args:

   816→        Returns:

   828→    def get_paper_by_id(self, paper_id: str) -> PeerReadPaper | None:

   849→    def query_papers(
   850→        self,
   851→        venue: str | None = None,
   852→        min_reviews: int = 1,
   853→        limit: int | None = None,
   854→    ) -> list[PeerReadPaper]:
   855→        """Query papers with filters.
   856→
   857→        Args:
   858→            venue: Filter by venue (None for all venues).
   859→            min_reviews: Minimum number of reviews required.
   860→            limit: Maximum number of papers to return.
   861→
   862→        Returns:
   863→            List of filtered PeerReadPaper models.
   864→        """
   865→        all_papers: list[PeerReadPaper] = []
   866→        venues_to_search = [venue] if venue else self.config.venues
   867→
   868→        for search_venue in venues_to_search:
   869→            for split in self.config.splits:
   870→                try:
   871→                    papers = self.load_papers(search_venue, split)
   872→                    all_papers.extend(papers)
   873→                except Exception as e:
   874→                    logger.warning(f"Failed to load {search_venue}/{split}: {e}")
   875→                    continue
   876→
   877→        # Apply filters
   878→        filtered_papers = [paper for paper in all_papers if len(paper.reviews) >= min_reviews]
   879→
   880→        # Apply limit
   881→        if limit:
   882→            filtered_papers = filtered_papers[:limit]
   883→
   884→        return filtered_papers

1→"""
     2→PeerRead dataset data models.
     3→
     4→This module defines Pydantic models for representing PeerRead scientific paper
     5→review data structures. These models ensure type safety and validation for
     6→papers, reviews, and evaluation results used in the multi-agent system evaluation.
     7→
     8→The models are based on the actual PeerRead dataset structure validated from:
     9→https://raw.githubusercontent.com/allenai/PeerRead/master/data/acl_2017/train/reviews/104.json
    10→
    11→This module also includes structured data models for LLM-generated reviews,
    12→ensuring consistency and validation against the PeerRead format.
    13→"""
    14→
    15→import re
    16→from typing import Annotated, Any, Literal
    17→
    18→from pydantic import BaseModel, BeforeValidator, ConfigDict, Field, field_validator
    19→
    20→# Coerce numeric score values from raw PeerRead JSON (int) to str.
    21→# Reason: Some PeerRead JSON files store scores as integers (e.g., "SOUNDNESS_CORRECTNESS": 3)
    22→# which fail str validation without coercion.
    23→_ScoreStr = Annotated[str, BeforeValidator(str)]
    24→
    25→# Recommendation word → numeric score mapping for weak-structured-output providers (e.g. Cerebras).
    26→_WORD_TO_SCORE: dict[str, int] = {
    27→    "strong accept": 5,
    28→    "strong_accept": 5,
    29→    "accept": 4,
    30→    "borderline accept": 3,
    31→    "borderline reject": 3,
    32→    "borderline": 3,
    33→    "reject": 2,
    34→    "strong reject": 1,
    35→    "strong_reject": 1,
    36→}
    37→
    38→
    39→def _coerce_score_to_int(v: Any) -> Any:
    40→    """Coerce LLM score values to int for providers that ignore integer schema constraints.
    41→
    42→    Reason: Providers like Cerebras with openai_supports_strict_tool_definition=False
    43→    may return natural language descriptions, floats, or word labels instead of integers.
    44→    Extraction priority: word mapping → float rounding → first digit in text → default 3.
    45→    """
    46→    if isinstance(v, int):
    47→        return v
    48→    if isinstance(v, float):
    49→        return max(1, min(5, round(v)))
    50→    if isinstance(v, str):
    51→        v_lower = v.lower().strip()
    52→        if v_lower in _WORD_TO_SCORE:
    53→            return _WORD_TO_SCORE[v_lower]
    54→        try:
    55→            return max(1, min(5, round(float(v_lower.split()[0]))))
    56→        except (ValueError, IndexError):
    57→            pass
    58→        if m := re.search(r"\b([1-5])\b", v):
    59→            return int(m.group(1))
    60→        return 3
    61→    return v
    62→

    64→def _coerce_presentation_format(v: Any) -> Any:
    65→    """Coerce presentation format to Literal['Poster', 'Oral'].
    66→
    67→    Reason: Same provider compliance issue — model may return a sentence describing
    68→    the format instead of the exact literal value.
    69→    """
    70→    if isinstance(v, str) and v not in ("Poster", "Oral"):
    71→        return "Oral" if "oral" in v.lower() else "Poster"
    72→    return v
    73→
    74→
    75→_ScoreInt = Annotated[int, BeforeValidator(_coerce_score_to_int)]
    76→_PresentationFormatLiteral = Annotated[
    77→    Literal["Poster", "Oral"], BeforeValidator(_coerce_presentation_format)
    78→]

    81→class PeerReadReview(BaseModel):
    82→    """Individual peer review from PeerRead dataset.
    83→
    84→    Note: Some PeerRead papers (e.g., 304-308, 330) lack optional fields.
    85→    Defaults to "UNKNOWN" for missing review criteria fields.
    86→
    87→    Accepts both PeerRead uppercase keys (IMPACT) and model lowercase keys
    88→    (impact) via populate_by_name with aliases. Numeric score fields are
    89→    coerced to str to handle raw PeerRead JSON integer values.
    90→    """
    91→
    92→    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True)
    93→
    94→    impact: _ScoreStr = Field(
    95→        default="UNKNOWN", validation_alias="IMPACT", description="Impact score (1-5)"
    96→    )
    97→    substance: _ScoreStr = Field(
    98→        default="UNKNOWN", validation_alias="SUBSTANCE", description="Substance score (1-5)"
    99→    )
   100→    appropriateness: _ScoreStr = Field(
   101→        default="UNKNOWN",
   102→        validation_alias="APPROPRIATENESS",
   103→        description="Appropriateness score (1-5)",
   104→    )
   105→    meaningful_comparison: _ScoreStr = Field(
   106→        default="UNKNOWN",
   107→        validation_alias="MEANINGFUL_COMPARISON",
   108→        description="Meaningful comparison score (1-5)",
   109→    )
   110→    presentation_format: str = Field(
   111→        default="Poster",
   112→        validation_alias="PRESENTATION_FORMAT",
   113→        description="Presentation format (Poster/Oral)",
   114→    )
   115→    comments: str = Field(default="", description="Detailed review comments")
   116→    soundness_correctness: _ScoreStr = Field(
   117→        default="UNKNOWN",
   118→        validation_alias="SOUNDNESS_CORRECTNESS",
   119→        description="Soundness/correctness score (1-5)",
   120→    )
   121→    originality: _ScoreStr = Field(
   122→        default="UNKNOWN", validation_alias="ORIGINALITY", description="Originality score (1-5)"
   123→    )
   124→    recommendation: _ScoreStr = Field(
   125→        default="UNKNOWN",
   126→        validation_alias="RECOMMENDATION",
   127→        description="Overall recommendation score (1-5)",
   128→    )
   129→    clarity: _ScoreStr = Field(
   130→        default="UNKNOWN", validation_alias="CLARITY", description="Clarity score (1-5)"
   131→    )
   132→    reviewer_confidence: _ScoreStr = Field(
   133→        default="UNKNOWN",
   134→        validation_alias="REVIEWER_CONFIDENCE",
   135→        description="Reviewer confidence score (1-5)",
   136→    )
   137→    is_meta_review: bool | None = Field(default=None, description="Whether this is a meta review")
   138→
   139→    def is_compliant(self) -> bool:
   140→        """Check if all score fields are populated (not UNKNOWN).
   141→
   142→        A review is compliant when every field that defaults to UNKNOWN
   143→        has been populated with an actual value from the raw JSON.
   144→
   145→        Returns:
   146→            True if all score fields have non-UNKNOWN values.
   147→        """
   148→        # Reason: Derive dynamically from model_fields to stay in sync with field definitions.
   149→        return all(
   150→            getattr(self, name) != "UNKNOWN"
   151→            for name, info in PeerReadReview.model_fields.items()
   152→            if info.default == "UNKNOWN"
   153→        )

    93→def resilient_tool_wrapper(tool: Tool[Any]) -> Tool[Any]:
    94→    """Wrap a PydanticAI Tool so HTTP and network errors return error strings.
    95→
    96→    Search tools are supplementary — when they fail, the agent should receive a
    97→    descriptive error message and continue generating output from paper content
    98→    and model knowledge. This prevents a search outage from crashing the run.
    99→
   100→    Catches:
   101→        - httpx.HTTPStatusError (403 Forbidden, 429 Too Many Requests, etc.)
   102→        - httpx.HTTPError (broader httpx network errors)
   103→        - Exception (any other network or library failure)
   104→
   105→    Args:
   106→        tool: The original PydanticAI Tool to wrap.
   107→
   108→    Returns:
   109→        A new Tool with the same name and description, but with a resilient
   110→        function that catches search errors and returns a descriptive string.
   111→    """
   112→    original_fn: Callable[..., Any] = tool.function
   113→
   114→    async def _resilient(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> Any:
   115→        try:
   116→            return await original_fn(*args, **kwargs)
   117→        except httpx.HTTPStatusError as exc:
   118→            status = exc.response.status_code
   119→            url = str(exc.request.url) if exc.request else "unknown"
   120→            logger.warning(f"Search tool '{tool.name}' HTTP {status} error for URL {url}: {exc}")
   121→            return (
   122→                f"Search tool '{tool.name}' is currently unavailable "
   123→                f"(HTTP {status}). Proceed using paper content and model knowledge."
   124→            )
   125→        except httpx.HTTPError as exc:
   126→            logger.warning(f"Search tool '{tool.name}' network error: {exc}")
   127→            return (
   128→                f"Search tool '{tool.name}' is currently unavailable "
   129→                f"(network error). Proceed using paper content and model knowledge."
   130→            )
   131→        except Exception as exc:
   132→            logger.warning(f"Search tool '{tool.name}' failed: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}")
   133→            return (
   134→                f"Search tool '{tool.name}' is currently unavailable "
   135→                f"({type(exc).__name__}). Proceed using paper content and model knowledge."
   136→            )
   137→
   138→    return Tool(
   139→        _resilient,
   140→        name=tool.name,
   141→        description=tool.description,
   142→    )
   143→

   145→def _validate_model_return(
   146→    result_output: Any,
   147→    result_model: type[ResultBaseType],
   148→) -> ResultBaseType:
   149→    """Validates the output against the expected model.
   150→
   151→    When result_output is a str (e.g. from OpenAI-compatible providers that
   152→    return plain text instead of structured output), tries model_validate_json()
   153→    first. This correctly handles valid JSON strings that model_validate() would
   154→    reject as "not a dict". Invalid JSON strings raise with the original content
   155→    included in the error message for easier debugging.
   156→
   157→    When result_output is a dict or already the correct Pydantic type,
   158→    model_validate() is used as before.
   159→
   160→    Args:
   161→        result_output: The output to validate. May be a JSON string, dict, or
   162→            existing Pydantic model instance.
   163→        result_model: The Pydantic model class to validate against.
   164→
   165→    Returns:
   166→        A validated instance of result_model.
   167→
   168→    Raises:
   169→        ValidationError: If the input cannot be parsed into result_model.
   170→        Exception: For unexpected errors during validation.
   171→    """
   172→    try:
   173→        if isinstance(result_output, str):
   174→            # Reason: model_validate() rejects str inputs even when valid JSON;
   175→            # model_validate_json() handles the JSON string path correctly.
   176→            try:
   177→                return result_model.model_validate_json(result_output)
   178→            except ValidationError as e:
   179→                msg = invalid_data_model_format(
   180→                    f"JSON parsing failed for input '{result_output}': {e}"
   181→                )
   182→                logger.error(msg)
   183→                raise ValueError(msg) from e
   184→        return result_model.model_validate(result_output)
   185→    except (ValidationError, ValueError):
   186→        raise
   187→    except Exception as e:
   188→        msg = generic_exception(str(e))
   189→        logger.exception(msg)
   190→        raise Exception(msg)
   191→

   193→async def _execute_traced_delegation(
   194→    sub_agent: Agent[None, BaseModel],
   195→    ctx: RunContext[None],
   196→    query: str,
   197→    *,
   198→    to_agent: str,
   199→    tool_name: str,
   200→    task_type: str,
   201→) -> Any:
   202→    """Execute a sub-agent delegation with trace collection.
   203→
   204→    Centralizes the tracing pattern shared by all delegation tools:
   205→    log coordination event, log interaction, run sub-agent, log tool call with timing.
   206→
   207→    Args:
   208→        sub_agent: The sub-agent to delegate to.
   209→        ctx: The run context from the manager agent.
   210→        query: The query string to delegate.
   211→        to_agent: Target agent name for trace logging.
   212→        tool_name: Tool name for trace logging.
   213→        task_type: Task type for trace logging.
   214→
   215→    Returns:
   216→        The AgentRunResult from the sub-agent execution.
   217→    """
   218→    trace_collector = get_trace_collector()
   219→    start_time = time.perf_counter()
   220→
   221→    trace_collector.log_agent_interaction(
   222→        from_agent="manager",
   223→        to_agent=to_agent,
   224→        interaction_type="delegation",
   225→        data={"query": query, "task_type": task_type},
   226→    )
   227→
   228→    trace_collector.log_coordination_event(
   229→        manager_agent="manager",
   230→        event_type="delegation",
   231→        target_agents=[to_agent],
   232→        data={"query": query, "task_type": task_type},
   233→    )
   234→
   235→    result = await sub_agent.run(query, usage=ctx.usage)
   236→
   237→    duration = time.perf_counter() - start_time
   238→    trace_collector.log_tool_call(
   239→        agent_id="manager",
   240→        tool_name=tool_name,
   241→        success=True,
   242→        duration=duration,
   243→        context=f"{task_type}_delegation",
   244→    )
   245→
   246→    return result
   247→

   249→def _add_research_tool(

   254→    """Add research delegation tool to manager agent.
   255→
   256→    Auto-instrumented by logfire.instrument_pydantic_ai() - no manual decorators needed.

   258→
   259→    @manager_agent.tool
   260→    async def delegate_research(  # type: ignore[reportUnusedFunction]
   261→        ctx: RunContext[None], query: str
   262→    ) -> ResearchResult | ResearchResultSimple | ReviewGenerationResult:
   263→        """Delegate a research task to the ResearchAgent. Returns structured research findings.

   265→        Args:
   266→            query: A natural-language description of what to research. Must be a plain
   267→                text string, NOT a JSON object or structured data.

   269→        result = await _execute_traced_delegation(

   277→        if isinstance(

   353→    synthesis_agent: Agent[None, BaseModel] | None = None,
   354→    result_type: type[
   355→        ResearchResult | ResearchResultSimple | ReviewGenerationResult
   356→    ] = ResearchResult,
   357→):
   358→    """
   359→    Adds tools to the manager agent for delegating tasks to research, analysis, and
   360→        synthesis agents.

   362→        manager_agent (Agent): The manager agent to which tools will be added.
   363→        research_agent (Agent): The agent responsible for handling research tasks.
   364→        analysis_agent (Agent, optional): The agent responsible for handling
   365→            analysis tasks. Defaults to None.
   366→        synthesis_agent (Agent, optional): The agent responsible for handling
   367→            synthesis tasks. Defaults to None.
   368→    Returns:
   369→        None
   370→    """
   371→    if research_agent is not None:
   372→        _add_research_tool(manager_agent, research_agent, result_type)
   373→
   374→    if analysis_agent is not None:

   381→def _create_agent(agent_config: AgentConfig) -> Agent[None, BaseModel]:
   382→    """Factory for creating configured agents."""

   384→        model=agent_config.model,
   385→        output_type=agent_config.output_type,

   392→def _create_optional_agent(
   393→    model: Any,
   394→    output_type: type[BaseModel],
   395→    system_prompt: str,
   396→    tools: list[Tool[Any]] | None = None,
   397→) -> Agent[None, BaseModel] | None:
   398→    """Create an agent if model is provided, otherwise return None.
   399→
   400→    Args:
   401→        model: The model instance, or None to skip creation.
   402→        output_type: Pydantic model type for agent output.
   403→        system_prompt: System prompt string for the agent.
   404→        tools: Optional list of tools to register on the agent.
   405→
   406→    Returns:
   407→        Configured Agent instance, or None if model is None.
   408→    """
   409→    if model is None:
   410→        return None
   411→    config: dict[str, Any] = {
   412→        "model": model,
   413→        "output_type": output_type,
   414→        "system_prompt": system_prompt,
   415→    }
   416→    if tools:
   417→        config["tools"] = tools
   418→    return _create_agent(AgentConfig.model_validate(config))

   421→def _get_result_type(
   422→    provider: str,
   423→    enable_review_tools: bool = False,
   424→) -> type[ResearchResult | ResearchResultSimple | ReviewGenerationResult]:
   425→    """
   426→    Select appropriate result model based on provider and tool configuration.
   427→
   428→    Args:
   429→        provider: The provider name (e.g., 'gemini', 'openai', etc.)
   430→        enable_review_tools: Whether review tools are enabled for paper reviews
   431→
   432→    Returns:
   433→        ReviewGenerationResult when review tools are enabled
   434→        ResearchResultSimple for Gemini (no additionalProperties support)
   435→        ResearchResult for other providers (supports flexible union types)
   436→    """
   437→    # When review tools are enabled, always use ReviewGenerationResult
   438→    if enable_review_tools:
   439→        return ReviewGenerationResult
   440→
   441→    # For research tasks, select based on provider capabilities
   442→    # Gemini doesn't support additionalProperties in JSON schema
   443→    if provider.lower() == "gemini":
   444→        return ResearchResultSimple
   445→    return ResearchResult

   448→def _create_manager(
   449→    prompts: dict[str, str],
   450→    models: ModelDict,
   451→    provider: str,
   452→    enable_review_tools: bool = False,
   453→    max_content_length: int = 15000,
   454→) -> Agent[None, BaseModel]:
   455→    """
   456→    Creates and configures a manager Agent with associated researcher, analyst,
   457→    and optionally synthesiser agents.
   458→    Args:
   459→        prompts (Dict[str, str]): Dictionary containing system prompts for each agent.
   460→        model_manager (GeminiModel | OpenAIModel): Model to be used by the manager
   461→            agent.
   462→        model_researcher (GeminiModel | OpenAIModel | None, optional): Model to be used
   463→            by the researcher agent.
   464→        model_analyst (GeminiModel | OpenAIModel | None, optional): Model to be used by
   465→            the analyst agent. Defaults to None.
   466→        model_synthesiser (GeminiModel | OpenAIModel | None, optional): Model to be used
   467→            by the synthesiser agent. Defaults to None.
   468→    Returns:
   469→        Agent: Configured manager agent with associated tools and agents.
   470→    """
   471→
   472→    status = f"Creating manager({models.model_manager.model_name})"
   473→    active_agents = [
   474→        agent
   475→        for agent in [
   476→            f"researcher({models.model_researcher.model_name})"
   477→            if models.model_researcher
   478→            else None,
   479→            f"analyst({models.model_analyst.model_name})" if models.model_analyst else None,
   480→            f"synthesiser({models.model_synthesiser.model_name})"
   481→            if models.model_synthesiser
   482→            else None,
   483→        ]
   484→        if agent
   485→    ]
   486→    status += f" with agents: {', '.join(active_agents)}" if active_agents else ""
   487→    logger.info(status)
   488→
   489→    # Select appropriate result type based on provider and tool configuration
   490→    result_type = _get_result_type(provider, enable_review_tools)
   491→
   492→    manager = _create_agent(
   493→        AgentConfig.model_validate(
   494→            {
   495→                "model": models.model_manager,
   496→                "output_type": result_type,
   497→                "system_prompt": prompts["system_prompt_manager"],
   498→            }
   499→        )
   500→    )
   501→
   502→    # Reason: prompt lookup guarded by model presence to match original behavior —
   503→    # tests may omit sub-agent prompt keys when model is None.
   504→    researcher = _create_optional_agent(
   505→        models.model_researcher,
   506→        result_type,
   507→        prompts["system_prompt_researcher"] if models.model_researcher else "",
   508→        tools=[resilient_tool_wrapper(duckduckgo_search_tool())],
   509→    )
   510→    analyst = _create_optional_agent(
   511→        models.model_analyst,
   512→        AnalysisResult,
   513→        prompts["system_prompt_analyst"] if models.model_analyst else "",
   514→    )
   515→    synthesiser = _create_optional_agent(
   516→        models.model_synthesiser,
   517→        AnalysisResult,
   518→        prompts["system_prompt_synthesiser"] if models.model_synthesiser else "",
   519→    )
   520→
   521→    _add_tools_to_manager_agent(manager, researcher, analyst, synthesiser, result_type)
   522→
   523→    # Determine target agent for PeerRead tools
   524→    # Researcher gets tools in multi-agent mode, manager in single-agent mode
   525→    target_agent = researcher if researcher is not None else manager
   526→    target_agent_id = "researcher" if researcher is not None else "manager"
   527→
   528→    # Add PeerRead base tools
   529→    add_peerread_tools_to_agent(target_agent, agent_id=target_agent_id)
   530→
   531→    # Add review tools if enabled
   532→    if enable_review_tools:
   533→        from app.tools.peerread_tools import add_peerread_review_tools_to_agent
   534→
   535→        add_peerread_review_tools_to_agent(
   536→            target_agent, agent_id=target_agent_id, max_content_length=max_content_length
   537→        )
   538→
   539→    return manager

   586→def _extract_rate_limit_detail(error: ModelHTTPError) -> str:
   587→    """Extract a human-readable detail message from a 429 ModelHTTPError body.
   588→
   589→    Args:
   590→        error: The 429 ModelHTTPError to extract detail from.
   591→
   592→    Returns:
   593→        str: Detail message from the error body, or string representation of error.
   594→    """
   595→    body = error.body if isinstance(error.body, dict) else {}
   596→    return body.get("message") or body.get("details") or str(error)  # type: ignore[return-value]
   597→
   598→
   599→def _handle_model_http_error(error: ModelHTTPError, provider: str, model_name: str) -> NoReturn:
   600→    """Handle non-429 ModelHTTPError with actionable logging. Re-raises the error.
   601→
   602→    For 429 rate-limit errors, callers should handle logging and SystemExit directly.
   603→    This allows sweep runners to catch ModelHTTPError for retry logic.
   604→
   605→    Args:
   606→        error: The ModelHTTPError to handle (expected: non-429).
   607→        provider: Provider name for logging context.
   608→        model_name: Model name for logging context.
   609→
   610→    Raises:
   611→        ModelHTTPError: Always re-raises the original error.
   612→    """
   613→    logger.error(f"HTTP error from model {provider}({model_name}): {error}")
   614→    raise error

   617→async def run_manager(
   618→    manager: Agent[None, BaseModel],
   619→    query: UserPromptType,
   620→    provider: str,
   621→    usage_limits: UsageLimits | None,
   622→    execution_id: str | None = None,
   623→) -> tuple[str, Any]:
   624→    """Asynchronously run the manager with the given query and provider.
   625→
   626→    Auto-instrumented by logfire.instrument_pydantic_ai() - no manual decorators needed.
   627→
   628→    Args:
   629→        manager: The system agent responsible for running the query.
   630→        query: The query to be processed by the manager.
   631→        provider: The provider to be used for the query.
   632→        usage_limits: The usage limits to be applied during the query execution.
   633→        execution_id: Optional pre-generated execution ID. When provided, used
   634→            as-is; otherwise a new ``exec_{hex12}`` ID is generated.
   635→
   636→    Returns:
   637→        Tuple of (execution_id, manager_output) for trace retrieval and evaluation.
   638→    """
   639→    # Initialize trace collection
   640→    trace_collector = get_trace_collector()
   641→    if execution_id is None:
   642→        execution_id = f"exec_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}"
   643→    trace_collector.start_execution(execution_id)
   644→
   645→    model_obj = getattr(manager, "model", None)
   646→    model_name = (
   647→        model_obj
   648→        if isinstance(model_obj, str)
   649→        else (getattr(model_obj, "model_name", "unknown") if model_obj else "unknown")
   650→    )
   651→    logger.info(f"Researching with {provider}({model_name}) and Topic: {query} ...")
   652→
   653→    try:
   654→        logger.info("Waiting for model response ...")
   655→        # Narrow query type for PydanticAI Agent.run() compatibility
   656→        user_prompt: str | None = query if isinstance(query, str | None) else str(query)
   657→        result = await manager.run(user_prompt=user_prompt, usage_limits=usage_limits)
   658→        logger.info(f"Result: {result}")
   659→        logger.info(f"Usage statistics: {result.usage()}")
   660→
   661→        # Finalize trace collection
   662→        trace_collector.end_execution()
   663→        logger.info(f"Trace collection completed for execution: {execution_id}")
   664→
   665→        return execution_id, result.output
   666→
   667→    except ModelHTTPError as e:
   668→        trace_collector.end_execution()
   669→        if e.status_code == 429:
   670→            detail = _extract_rate_limit_detail(e)
   671→            logger.error(f"Rate limit exceeded for {provider}({model_name}): {detail}")
   672→            raise SystemExit(1) from e
   673→        _handle_model_http_error(e, provider, model_name)
   674→
   675→    except UsageLimitExceeded as e:
   676→        trace_collector.end_execution()
   677→        logger.error(f"Token limit reached for {provider}({model_name}): {e}")
   678→        raise SystemExit(1) from e
   679→
   680→    except Exception as e:
   681→        trace_collector.end_execution()
   682→        logger.error(f"Error in run_manager: {e}")
   683→        raise
   684→

   686→def _determine_effective_token_limit(
   687→    token_limit: int | None,
   688→    chat_env_config: AppEnv,
   689→    provider_config: ProviderConfig,
   690→) -> int | None:
   691→    """Determine effective token limit with priority: CLI/GUI > env var > config.
   692→
   693→    Args:
   694→        token_limit: Optional CLI/GUI token limit override
   695→        chat_env_config: App environment config with AGENT_TOKEN_LIMIT
   696→        provider_config: Provider config with usage_limits
   697→
   698→    Returns:
   699→        Effective token limit or None if not set
   700→    """
   701→    if token_limit is not None:
   702→        return token_limit
   703→    if chat_env_config.AGENT_TOKEN_LIMIT is not None:
   704→        return chat_env_config.AGENT_TOKEN_LIMIT
   705→    return provider_config.usage_limits
   706→
   707→
   708→def _validate_token_limit(effective_limit: int | None) -> None:
   709→    """Validate token limit bounds (1000-1000000).
   710→
   711→    Args:
   712→        effective_limit: Token limit to validate
   713→
   714→    Raises:
   715→        ValueError: If limit is outside valid range
   716→    """
   717→    if effective_limit is None:
   718→        return
   719→
   720→    if effective_limit < 1000:
   721→        msg = f"Token limit {effective_limit} below minimum 1000"
   722→        logger.error(msg)
   723→        raise ValueError(msg)
   724→
   725→    if effective_limit > 1000000:
   726→        msg = f"Token limit {effective_limit} above maximum 1000000"
   727→        logger.error(msg)
   728→        raise ValueError(msg)
   729→
   730→
   731→def _create_usage_limits(effective_limit: int | None) -> UsageLimits | None:
   732→    """Create UsageLimits object if token limit is set.
   733→
   734→    Args:
   735→        effective_limit: Effective token limit
   736→
   737→    Returns:
   738→        UsageLimits object or None
   739→    """
   740→    if effective_limit is None:
   741→        return None
   742→    return UsageLimits(request_limit=10, total_tokens_limit=effective_limit)
   743→
